data.title: Rubicon Organics appoints chair after AGM, grants DSUs - meeting held on July 31, 2025. TSXV: ROMJ (OTCQX: ROMJF)
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia on July 31, 2025, Rubicon Organics Inc. (TSXV: ROMJ) (OTCQX: ROMJF), a licensed producer specializing in organic certified and premium cannabis, shared outcomes from its Annual General and Special Meeting of shareholders held recently. The meeting revealed crucial details regarding the company's strategic decisions.

Britain said the European Union will remove tariffs on key steel products under a quota system from Friday as part of a reset of ties and a recent deal to ease trade barriers.

data.title: Lincoln Property Company and NYLREI acquire 600 Townsend West, San Francisco, a 5-story Class A office building, 210,000 sq ft.
data.description: In San Francisco, Lincoln Property Company and New York Life Real Estate Investors have purchased 600 Townsend West, a top-tier Class A creative office building situated in the bustling Design District. This five-story building boasts approximately 210,000 square feet and is positioned amidst a thriving commercial zone, surrounded by expanding AI enterprises and other key elements propelling the city's tech industry. The property, situated at Townsend and 7th, offers a variety of outstanding amenities and features.

data.title: GolfStatus and Dormie Network give $10,000 to a charity golf event through Play It Forward campaign, supporting golf-driven missions.
data.description: In Lincoln, Nebraska, GolfStatus, Dormie Network, and Dormie Network Foundation have reintroduced the Play It Forward campaign. This initiative highlights nonprofit organizations leveraging golf to create a positive impact and advance their goals. The campaign will award a $10,000 donation to a deserving charity golf event in 2025. Nominations for the grant will be accepted from August 1 to September 30. The aim is to recognize and support organizations using golf for philanthropic efforts and community enrichment.

data.title: Roblox and Discord face lawsuit over alleged sexual exploitation of minor, claiming design flaws enabled predator. Shares falling.
data.description: Roblox Corporation shares are dropping after news of a lawsuit alleging both the gaming platform and Discord enabled the sexual exploitation of a minor, with the victim now transitioning to adulthood. The lawsuit claims the companies' platforms allowed a predator to target and abuse the plaintiff due to design flaws and lack of safety measures, underscoring systemic child safety failures. Roblox states they are committed to community safety, investing in advanced safety tech like machine learning and human moderation teams, operating 24/7 to detect and address inappropriate content and behavior. They aim to protect children by preventing personal information sharing or directing users off-platform to less stringent safety standards. Full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Casella Waste Systems, Inc. has priced $29.0 million of Finance Authority of Maine Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds for remarketing.
data.description: Casella Waste Systems, Inc. has announced the remarketing of $29.0 million aggregate principal amount of Finance Authority of Maine Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds originally issued in the aggregate principal amount of $30.0 million. $1.0 million of the Bonds will be redeemed on August 1, 2025, with the remaining $29.0 million being remarketed on the same date as Series 2015R-3. The Bonds, with a final maturity of August 1, 2035, will have a new interest rate of 5.000% per annum for a new interest rate period starting on August 1, 2025, and ending on July 31, 2035. The remarketing and redemption are expected to be effective on August 1, 2025.

data.title: Federal court ruling in favor of No Labels Party in Arizona allows independents access to run for office, revolutionizing state's political landscape.
data.description: In Phoenix, the No Labels Party of Arizona achieved a significant victory in federal court with the case No Labels Party of AZ v. Fontes. The ruling has reshaped the state's political landscape by granting independent and unaffiliated voters, as well as candidates, exceptional opportunities to participate in elections free from political party restrictions. The landmark decision by the 9th Circuit Court of Appeals has established the No Labels Party of Arizona as a legitimate choice for candidates to pursue political office, marking a pivotal moment in the state's political scene.

data.title: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon switches from skeptic to supporter, now backs stablecoins and blockchain tech.
data.description: Formerly a vocal critic of cryptocurrencies, Jamie Dimon has shifted his stance and now expresses belief in stablecoins. JPMorgan is significantly increasing its involvement in digital assets, marking a notable transition in Dimon's perspective on crypto.

data.title: Visa expands stablecoin features, now supporting 3 new tokens and 2 additional blockchains in latest move in crypto industry.
data.description: Visa has expanded its stablecoin settlement infrastructure to accommodate three additional tokens and two more blockchains in its recent move to embrace the crypto market.
